metadata.notes: agy writes each step only on completion (status DONE); intermediate prose is NOT split into no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE chunks, so the no-flicker invariant holds trivially (like gemini-cli, unlike codex). daemon=full; driver stubs wait_turn → driver-gap.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

Route: driver-gap (record ports the stubbed wait_turn seam, then drives).

## What the scenario pins

The no-flicker streaming invariant: across all partial flushes of a multi-sentence prose response the session stays solidly `working` and only flips to `ready` on the terminal turn-end signal — topology `ready → working → ready`, no tool calls, no premature `ready`.

## agent_supports: yes

The agy CLI produces a normal conversational turn for a 'write a 5-sentence story' prompt with no tool use — its default behavior, no flag needed.

## daemon_capability: full

This is the gemini-cli case, NOT the codex case. The key emission finding: agy persists each transcript step ONLY on completion (every line in both transcript.jsonl AND transcript_full.jsonl is status:"DONE" — verified across all real conversations). Crucially, intermediate assistant prose is NOT written as separate intermediate no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE records: every completed turn contains EXACTLY ONE no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE — the terminal sentinel — and its content is EMPTY (contentlen 0 observed at brain/69343e3e index 38 and 752dfc36 index 6). The parser emits `turn_done` only on a no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE (parser.go parsePlannerResponse, len(toolCalls)==0 branch). Since agy writes no mid-turn no-tool prose chunk, there is no intermediate record that could prematurely fire turn_done; the band stays `working` across the streaming window and flips to `ready` exactly once, on the terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE. The no-flicker invariant holds — daemon=full, recordable.

## driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

The recipe needs `wait_turn` (block until the terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE) but driver-interactive.sh declares DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" and stubs the wait_turn arm. recipe-lint flags it as a semantic_gap. `record` must port the turn-done poll (SEAM 2) before driving.
caveats: daemon=full is PROVISIONAL on emission until a live recording: it depends on agy writing the assistant prose as a single completed step with the terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE carrying empty content. Verified structurally on tool-heavy transcripts (one empty terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE per turn); record must live-confirm zero working→ready transitions across the streaming window on a genuinely long PROSE-only turn.
Tokens/cost are not in the JSONL (SQLite protobuf only); observations assert no cost/tokens.

sources.note: exactly one no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE per turn (index 38), EMPTY content — terminal sentinel; no intermediate no-tool prose chunks
even the unfiltered full transcript is 100% status:"DONE" — agy appends steps on completion only, never partial flushes
parsePlannerResponse: turn_done only when len(toolCalls)==0; a single such terminal line per turn means no streaming flicker
sibling daemon=full rationale: single-write-per-turn agents have no partial-content record to flicker on

script.text: Write a five-sentence story about a lighthouse keeper. Just prose, no tools, no lists.
